Modelos mecanicistas y estadísticos para brotes epidémicos: el caso de la COVID-19 en Euskadi

Nazioarteko Mintegia / Seminario Internacional Eustat 2020

Material del curso

19-20 de Noviembre

Código en codeshare https://codeshare.io/alwwNY

Cargar librerías

rm(list=ls())
library(lubridate) # Librería para fechas
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(deSolve)

Modelo SIR para datos de COVID-19

  • Cargamos la librería coronavirus
library(coronavirus)
?coronavirus
data(coronavirus)
head(coronavirus)
##         date province     country      lat     long      type cases
## 1 2020-01-22          Afghanistan 33.93911 67.70995 confirmed     0
## 2 2020-01-23          Afghanistan 33.93911 67.70995 confirmed     0
## 3 2020-01-24          Afghanistan 33.93911 67.70995 confirmed     0
## 4 2020-01-25          Afghanistan 33.93911 67.70995 confirmed     0
## 5 2020-01-26          Afghanistan 33.93911 67.70995 confirmed     0
## 6 2020-01-27          Afghanistan 33.93911 67.70995 confirmed     0
range(coronavirus$date)
## [1] "2020-01-22" "2020-07-31"
  • Veamos la estructura de los datos:
# Get top confirmed cases by state
coronavirus %>%
  filter(type == "confirmed") %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(cases)) %>%
  arrange(-total) %>%
  head(20)
## # A tibble: 20 x 2
##    country          total
##    <chr>            <int>
##  1 US             4562038
##  2 Brazil         2662485
##  3 India          1695988
##  4 Russia          838461
##  5 South Africa    493183
##  6 Mexico          424637
##  7 Peru            407492
##  8 Chile           355667
##  9 United Kingdom  304793
## 10 Iran            304204
## 11 Colombia        295508
## 12 Spain           288522
## 13 Pakistan        278305
## 14 Saudi Arabia    275905
## 15 Italy           247537
## 16 Bangladesh      237661
## 17 Turkey          230873
## 18 France          225197
## 19 Germany         210399
## 20 Argentina       191302
# Get the number of recovered cases in Australia by state
coronavirus %>%
  filter(type == "recovered", country == "Australia") %>%
  group_by(province) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(cases)) %>%
  arrange(-total)
## # A tibble: 8 x 2
##   province                     total
##   <chr>                        <int>
## 1 Victoria                      4479
## 2 New South Wales               2989
## 3 Queensland                    1066
## 4 Western Australia              650
## 5 South Australia                443
## 6 Tasmania                       216
## 7 Australian Capital Territory   110
## 8 Northern Territory              30
  • Vamos a analizar los casos de un país de la base de datos:

Nota:

  • Obsérvese que lo que se necesita son las personas actualmente infectadas (infectados acumulados menos los eliminados, es decir, recuperados o fallecidos). Sin embargo, el número de personas recuperadas es difícil de obtener y probablemente se subestime debido al sesgo de infra-reporte.
`%>%` <- magrittr::`%>%`

datos <- coronavirus %>%
  dplyr::filter(country == "Belgium") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(date, type) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(total = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = type,
    values_from = total
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(date) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(active = confirmed - death - recovered) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    confirmed_cum = cumsum(confirmed),
    death_cum = cumsum(death),
    recovered_cum = cumsum(recovered),
    active_cum = cumsum(active)
  )
  • Para ilustrar el modelo SIR sobre los datos de COVID-19, vamos a seleccionar un rango de fechas concreto (entre el 25 de febrero y el 26 de marzo) y a utilizar el número acumulado de personas infectadas, lo que probablemente no sea un problema en este caso, ya que el número de casos recuperados es insignificante en el momento del análisis.
sir_start_date <- "2020-02-04"
  sir_end_date <- "2020-03-30"
par(mfrow=c(2,1))
with(datos,{plot(date, confirmed, t = "h")})
abline(v = c(ymd(sir_start_date),ymd(sir_end_date)),col= 2)
with(datos,{plot(date, active_cum, t = "l")})
abline(v = c(ymd(sir_start_date),ymd(sir_end_date)),col= 2)

Infected <- subset(datos, date >= ymd(sir_start_date) & date <= ymd(sir_end_date))$active_cum
Day <- 1:length(Infected)
N <- 11515793 # Población en Belgica
SIR <- function(time, state, parameters) {
  par <- as.list(c(state, parameters))
  with(par, {
    dS <- -beta * I * S / N
    dI <- beta * I * S / N - gamma * I
    dR <- gamma * I
    list(c(dS, dI, dR))
  })
}
init <- c(S = N - Infected[1],I = Infected[1],R= 0)

RSS <- function(parameters){
  names(parameters) <- c("beta","gamma")
  out <- ode(y = init, times = Day, func = SIR, parms = parameters)
  fit <- out[,3]
  sum((Infected-fit)^2)
}

params_init <- c(0.5,0.5)
opt <- optim(params_init, RSS, method = "L-BFGS-B", lower = c(0,0), upper = c(1,1))
opt$message
## [1] "CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F <= FACTR*EPSMCH"
opt$par <- setNames(opt$par, c("beta","gamma"))
opt$par
##      beta     gamma 
## 0.5841185 0.4158816
fit <- data.frame(ode(y = init, times = Day, func = SIR, parms = opt$par))
head(fit)
##   time        S        I         R
## 1    1 11515792 1.000000 0.0000000
## 2    2 11515791 1.183217 0.4529126
## 3    3 11515791 1.400002 0.9888057
## 4    4 11515790 1.656506 1.6228840
## 5    5 11515789 1.960006 2.3731369
## 6    6 11515787 2.319112 3.2608467
Day <- 1:(length(Infected))
Dates <- seq(as.Date(sir_start_date),as.Date(sir_end_date),by = 1)

plot(Dates, Infected, type ="p",cex=.65)
lines(Dates,fit$I,col=2)

h = 30 # Horizonte de predicción a h días
Infected_long <- subset(datos, date >= ymd(sir_start_date) & date <= ymd(sir_end_date) + h)$active_cum
Day_long <- 1:length(Infected_long)
Dates_long <- seq(as.Date(sir_start_date),as.Date(sir_end_date)+h,by=1)

fit_long <- data.frame(ode(y = init, times = Day_long, func = SIR, parms = opt$par))
head(fit_long)
##   time        S        I         R
## 1    1 11515792 1.000000 0.0000000
## 2    2 11515791 1.183217 0.4529126
## 3    3 11515791 1.400002 0.9888057
## 4    4 11515790 1.656506 1.6228840
## 5    5 11515789 1.960006 2.3731369
## 6    6 11515787 2.319112 3.2608467
par(mfrow=c(2,1))
plot(Dates_long,Infected_long,xlab = "",cex=.5,pch=19)
lines(Dates_long,fit_long$I,col = 4,lwd=2)
lines(Dates,fit$I,col=3, lty = 2,lwd=2)
abline(v = ymd(sir_end_date), col= 2)
legend("topleft", c("Modelo SIR (ajuste)", "Modelo SIR (predicción)"), col = c("green","blue"),lty = 1:2,lwd=3, cex = .85)
plot(Dates_long,Infected_long,xlab = "",cex=.5,pch=19,log = "y")
## Warning in xy.coords(x, y, xlabel, ylabel, log): 13 y values <= 0 omitted from
## logarithmic plot
abline(v = ymd(sir_end_date), col= 2)
